summary.executive_summary: Potential issues identified in MCPLogger related to log file handling and exception logging.

findings.title: Log file directory creation may fail silently
findings.deep_dive: The code attempts to create the log directory using log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) but does not check if the operation succeeds. If the parent directories cannot be created (e.g., due to permission issues), the log file creation will fail silently, potentially causing runtime errors later.
findings.solution_explanation: Add explicit error handling around the directory creation. If the directory creation fails, raise a custom exception or log a critical error to alert the system administrator.
findings.reference: Python documentation on os.makedirs and exception handling best practices

findings.title: Exception logging may include sensitive information
findings.deep_dive: The exception method logs the exception with traceback using self.logger.exception(full_message). This can potentially include sensitive information in the traceback, such as file paths, variable values, or stack frames containing sensitive data.
findings.solution_explanation: Consider using a more controlled way to log exceptions, such as logging only the exception type and message without the full traceback. Alternatively, implement a mechanism to filter out sensitive information from the traceback before logging.
findings.reference: OWASP guidelines on logging sensitive information and Python logging documentation
